message:
refactor(widgets): use enum for evidence descriptions to avoid allocations

Replaces String-based evidence descriptions with an EvidenceDescription
enum to eliminate heap allocations during scoring.

Changes:
- Add EvidenceDescription enum with variants for common description patterns
- Implement Display trait for human-readable output
- Update EvidenceEntry.description field type from String to EvidenceDescription
- Update EvidenceLedger::add() to accept EvidenceDescription directly
- Convert existing string descriptions to appropriate enum variants

Variants:
- Static(&'static str): For fixed messages like "query matches tag"
- TitleLengthChars { len }: For "title length N chars"
- FirstMatchPos { pos }: For "first match at position N"
- WordBoundaryCount { count }: For "N word boundary matches"
- GapTotal { total }: For "total gap of N characters"
- CoveragePercent { percent }: For "query covers N% of title"

Benefits:
- Zero heap allocations for common evidence types
- Lazy formatting only when description is actually displayed
- Type-safe evidence metadata (can be queried without parsing strings)
- Same Display output as before for compatibility

This optimization reduces allocator pressure during high-frequency
command palette filtering operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
